scope: TileLang sparse-MLA compressed dKV backward follow-up after P/dP recompute-once gate

code.read: The probe now has a concrete two-kernel P/dP slab pipeline and a fused grouped-D recompute+dKV mode that keeps P/dP on chip and reuses it across multiple output D blocks when shared memory allows.

baseline_context.read: The promotion gate is beating the current sparse-atomic compressed-dKV wall while avoiding materialized [S, topk, H] probability and grad-logit slabs.

results.s4096_topk512_split_panel_caveat: This lower number drops 333843 panel-overflow pairs because recompute modes do not support spill-overflow yet, so it is not the full actual-LI route.

failed_or_rejected_shapes.reason: TileLang dKV consumer requested 180224 bytes dynamic shared memory, above the MI350 limit of 163840 bytes.
TileLang layout inference failed with no available layout for the final-dKV consumer.

two_kernel_pdp_store_then_dkv.decision: Rejected as a speed path. The full no-overflow route is 16.24 ms, worse than the existing sparse-atomic wall and almost exactly the expected detached producer+consumer cost.

results.s4096_topk512_d32x2_caveat: This legal production-shape run uses split panels with 333843 overflow pairs dropped, so it is a fit/performance check rather than full-route correctness.

failed_or_rejected_shapes.reason: TileLang MFMA layout inference hit a divide-by-zero; block_d16 is not viable through this lowering path.
TileLang generated 303104 bytes dynamic shared memory, above the MI350 limit of 163840 bytes.
TileLang generated 290816 bytes dynamic shared memory, above the MI350 limit of 163840 bytes.

grouped_d_recompute_dkv.decision: Keep as a source-level prototype only. It proves the on-chip P/dP reuse contract works at S128/S512, but the only S4096 shape that fits is 61.64 ms and the useful reuse shapes exceed shared memory.

next_kernel_contract.read: The next TileLang attempt should not use a full max_active_groups x D grouped accumulator and should not write P/dP slabs. It needs a smaller warp/block segmented reducer keyed by PairSlots, or a multi-CTA compact partial scheme with a cheap reducer, so P/dP stays near the producer while global writes are reduced without blowing shared memory.
specific_next_steps: Replace the dense [max_active_groups, D] group_acc table with a small keyed/segmented accumulator for only groups touched by the current K tile.
Keep the no-overflow cap512 route as the full-route gate; treat split cap256 topk512 runs as fit checks until recompute spill-overflow exists.
If TileLang cannot express the segmented reducer without excessive shared memory or layout failures, move this exact contract to HIP/CK-Tile/rocWMMA rather than another detached slab pipeline.
